etymology.description: semantic 爿(爿) (pán, "bed; half of a tree trunk") + phonetic 曹(曹) (cáo) -> leader; general
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 爿(爿) (pán, "bed; half of a tree trunk") can imply division or separation, evoking leadership and strategy. The phonetic element 曹(曹) (cáo) contributes the sound. Together, these components symbolize guidance or leadership, used for a lead or command role.
etymology.simplification: 將 was simplified to 将 by changing 爿(爿) (pán) to a simpler component with fewer strokes.

parts.english: bed; half of a tree trunk

examples.chinese: 这位将军将带领军队(這位將軍將帶領軍隊)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi jiāngjūn jiāng dàilǐng jūnduì.
examples.english: This general will lead the army.

examples.chinese: 小船将离开港口(小船將離開港口)。
examples.english: The small boat will leave the harbor.

examples.chinese: 老师将安排新的课程计划(老師將安排新的課程計劃)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī jiāng ānpái xīn de kèchéng jìhuà.
examples.english: The teacher will arrange a new course plan.
